revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '吉祥院多聞寺（たもんじ）は、兵庫県神戸市北区にある真言宗の寺院。山号は六甲山。 本尊は秘仏[[毘沙門天]]・吉...'
revision.text:
吉祥院多聞寺（たもんじ）は、兵庫県神戸市北区にある真言宗の寺院。山号は六甲山。 本尊は秘仏[[毘沙門天]]・[[吉祥天]]・善弐師童子。前立ち本尊は毘沙門天である。
==歴史== この寺はインドの渡来僧、[[法道仙人]]によって[[孝徳天皇]]の頃に開かれたと伝えられる。その後、荒廃していた寺院を858年高野山の僧、長善阿舎利が復興した。伽藍は元は現在地の東南にある古寺山にあった。1180年に平清盛が神戸の福原に遷都した時、都の鬼門（北東）方向を守る寺院とした。寺領千町歩を与え、鞍馬、八瀬の大原から人々を移住させたために、この唐櫃地区には近代まで他とは異なる独特の風習が伝わっていた。 源平の合戦の際、源義経が須磨の一の谷を攻める時、多聞寺がその道案内を断ったことを理由に、焼き払われた。その後、廃虚になっていたのを1462年古寺山から現在地に移転・再建された。現在の本堂は1690年に了円が建て直したものである。 かつては毎年2月11日に催される「お塔祭」は盛大に行われていた。このお塔祭をを一、唐櫃の村を取り仕切るのは四鬼家であった。四鬼家ははるか昔、役行者の命によって奈良県吉野郡天川村洞川から、この唐櫃に移り住んだといわれ、唐櫃村と西六甲の山を管理し、同時に六甲修験の山伏達を管理していた。==多聞寺奥の院== [[六甲山]]山頂尾根付近の雲が岩・心経岩・[[六甲比命神社]]（祭神[[弁財天]]）を奥の院とする。雲が岩で法道仙人が修行中、紫色の雲に乗った[[毘沙門天]]と出会った、と伝わる。
==所在地== 兵庫県神戸市北区唐櫃
==関連項目== *六甲比命神社 *法道仙人 *四鬼家 *六甲修験[http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm]
==出典・注釈== {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

==歴史== この寺はインドの渡来僧、[[法道仙人]]によって[[孝徳天皇]]の頃に開かれたと伝えられる。その後、荒廃していた寺院を858年[[高野山]]の僧、長善阿舎利が復興した。伽藍は元は現在地の東南にある古寺山にあった。1180年に[[平清盛]]が神戸の[[福原京|福原]]に遷都した時、京都の[[鞍馬山]]に擬して都の鬼門（北東）方向を守る寺院とした。<ref>北神戸歴史の道 野村貴郎著 神戸新聞総合出版センター刊</ref>寺領千町歩を与え、鞍馬、八瀬の大原から人々を移住させたために、この唐櫃地区には近代まで他とは異なる独特の風習が伝わっていた。
[[源平の合戦]]の際、[[源義経]]が須磨の一の谷を攻める時、多聞寺がその道案内を断ったことを理由に焼き払われた。その後、廃虚になっていたのを1462年古寺山から現在地に移転・再建された。現在の本堂は1690年に了円が建て直したものである。
かつては毎年2月11日に催される「お塔祭」は盛大に行われていた。このお塔祭をはじめ、唐櫃の村を取り仕切るのは四鬼家であった。四鬼家ははるか昔、[[役行者]]の命によって[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[天川村]]洞川から、この唐櫃に移り住んだといわれ、唐櫃村と西六甲の山を管理し、同時に六甲修験の山伏達を管理していた。

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 吉祥院多聞寺 |画像 = |所在地 = 兵庫県神戸市北区有野町唐櫃 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |山号 = 六甲山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]]<ref name="arima">{{Cite book|和書|author = 有馬郡|authorlink = |title = 有馬郡誌|url = http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1174068|format = |accessdate = 2014-01-22|date = 1929-09-10|publisher = |location =|volume = 上|page = 445|chapter = 第三章 寺院|ref = harv}}</ref> |寺格 = 高野山谷上西禅寺末<ref name="arima" /> |本尊 = 毘沙門天 |創建年 = [[孝徳天皇]]の頃（645〜654年） |開山 = 伝 [[法道仙人]] |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} '''吉祥院多聞寺'''（たもんじ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[北区 (神戸市)|北区]]にある[[真言宗]]の寺院。山号は六甲山。本尊は秘仏[[毘沙門天]]・[[吉祥天]]・善弐師童子。前立ち本尊は毘沙門天である。
==歴史== この寺はインドの渡来僧、[[法道仙人]]によって[[孝徳天皇]]の頃に開かれたと伝えられる。その後、荒廃していた寺院を858年[[高野山]]の僧、長善阿舎利が復興した。伽藍は元は現在地の東南にある古寺山にあった。1180年に[[平清盛]]が神戸の[[福原京|福原]]に遷都した時、京都の[[鞍馬山]]に擬して都の鬼門（北東）方向を守る寺院とした。<ref>北神戸歴史の道 野村貴郎著 神戸新聞総合出版センター刊</ref>寺領千町歩を与え、鞍馬、八瀬の大原から人々を移住させたために、この唐櫃地区には近代まで他とは異なる独特の風習が伝わっていた。
[[源平の合戦]]の際、[[源義経]]が須磨の一の谷を攻める時、多聞寺がその道案内を断ったことを理由に焼き払われた。その後、廃虚になっていたのを1462年古寺山から現在地に移転・再建された。現在の本堂は1690年に了円が建て直したものである。
かつては毎年2月11日に催される「お塔祭」は盛大に行われていた。このお塔祭をはじめ、唐櫃の村を取り仕切るのは四鬼家であった。四鬼家ははるか昔、[[役行者]]の命によって[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[天川村]]洞川から、この唐櫃に移り住んだといわれ、唐櫃村と西六甲の山を管理し、同時に六甲修験の山伏達を管理していた。
==多聞寺奥の院== [[六甲山]]山頂尾根付近の雲が岩・心経岩・[[六甲比命神社]]（祭神[[弁財天]]）を奥の院とする。雲が岩で法道仙人が修行中、紫色の雲に乗った[[毘沙門天]]と出会った、と伝わる。
==所在地== 〒651-1331 兵庫県神戸市北区有野町唐櫃

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 吉祥院多聞寺 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神戸市}} |所在地 = 兵庫県神戸市北区有野町唐櫃 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |山号 = 六甲山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]]<ref name="arima">{{Cite book|和書|author = 有馬郡|authorlink = |title = 有馬郡誌|url = {{NDLDC|1174068}}|format = |accessdate = 2014-01-22|date = 1929-09-10|publisher = |location =|volume = 上|page = 445|chapter = 第三章 寺院|ref = harv}}</ref> |寺格 = 高野山谷上西禅寺末<ref name="arima" /> |本尊 = 毘沙門天 |創建年 = [[孝徳天皇]]の頃（645〜654年） |開山 = 伝 [[法道仙人]] |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }}
'''吉祥院多聞寺'''（たもんじ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[北区 (神戸市)|北区]]にある[[真言宗]]の寺院。山号は六甲山。本尊は秘仏[[毘沙門天]]・[[吉祥天]]・善弐師童子。前立ち本尊は毘沙門天である。
==歴史== この寺はインドの渡来僧、[[法道仙人]]によって[[孝徳天皇]]の頃に開かれたと伝えられる。その後、荒廃していた寺院を858年[[高野山]]の僧、長善阿舎利が復興した。伽藍は元は現在地の東南にある古寺山にあった。1180年に[[平清盛]]が神戸の[[福原京|福原]]に遷都した時、京都の[[鞍馬山]]に擬して都の鬼門（北東）方向を守る寺院とした。<ref>北神戸歴史の道 野村貴郎著 神戸新聞総合出版センター刊</ref>寺領千町歩を与え、鞍馬、八瀬の大原から人々を移住させたために、この唐櫃地区には近代まで他とは異なる独特の風習が伝わっていた。
[[治承・寿永の乱]]（源平合戦）の際、[[源義経]]が須磨の一の谷を攻める時、多聞寺がその道案内を断ったことを理由に焼き払われた。その後、廃虚になっていたのを1462年古寺山から現在地に移転・再建された。現在の本堂は1690年に了円が建て直したものである。
かつては毎年2月11日に催される「お塔祭」は盛大に行われていた。このお塔祭をはじめ、唐櫃の村を取り仕切るのは四鬼家であった。四鬼家ははるか昔、[[役行者]]の命によって[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[天川村]]洞川から、この唐櫃に移り住んだといわれ、唐櫃村と西六甲の山を管理し、同時に六甲修験の山伏達を管理していた。
==多聞寺奥の院== [[六甲山]]山頂尾根付近の仰臥岩・雲が岩(紫雲賀岩）・[[六甲比命神社]]（祭神[[弁財天]]）・心経岩を奥の院とする。雲が岩で法道仙人が修行中、紫色の雲に乗った[[毘沙門天]]と出会った、と伝わる。
==出典・注釈== {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

[[善弐師童子]]
|創建年 = [[孝徳天皇]]の頃（645〜654年） |開山 = 伝 [[法道仙人]] |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 六甲山吉祥院多聞寺 |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} '''吉祥院多聞寺'''（たもんじ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[北区 (神戸市)|北区]][[有野町]][[有野町唐櫃|唐櫃]]にある[[真言宗]]の寺院。山号は六甲山。本尊は秘仏[[毘沙門天]]・[[吉祥天]]・善弐師童子。前立ち本尊は毘沙門天である。 == 歴史 == この寺はインドの渡来僧、[[法道仙人]]によって[[孝徳天皇]]の頃に開かれたと伝えられる。その後、荒廃していた寺院を858年[[高野山]]の僧、長善阿舎利が復興した。伽藍は元は現在地の東南にある古寺山にあった。1180年に[[平清盛]]が神戸の[[福原京|福原]]に遷都した時、京都の[[鞍馬山]]に擬して都の鬼門（北東）方向を守る寺院とした。北神戸歴史の道　野村貴郎著　神戸新聞総合出版センター刊寺領千町歩を与え、鞍馬、八瀬の大原から人々を移住させたために、この唐櫃地区には近代まで他とは異なる独特の風習が伝わっていた。　 [[治承・寿永の乱]]（源平合戦）の際、[[源義経]]が須磨の一の谷を攻める時、多聞寺がその道案内を断ったことを理由に焼き払われた。その後、廃虚になっていたのを1462年古寺山から現在地に移転・再建された。現在の本堂は1690年に了円が建て直したものである。 かつては毎年2月11日に催される「お塔祭」は盛大に行われていた。このお塔祭をはじめ、[[唐櫃村|唐櫃]]の村を取り仕切るのは[[四鬼家]]であった。四鬼家ははるか昔、[[役行者]]の命によって[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[天川村]][[洞川 (吉野郡天川村)|洞川]]から、この唐櫃に移り住んだといわれ、唐櫃村と西六甲の山を管理し、同時に六甲修験の山伏達を管理していた。 == 多聞寺奥の院 == [[六甲山]]山頂尾根付近の仰臥岩・雲が岩(紫雲賀岩）・[[六甲比命神社]]（祭神[[弁財天]]）・心経岩を奥の院とする。雲が岩で法道仙人が修行中、紫色の雲に乗った[[毘沙門天]]と出会った、と伝わる。 == 関連項目 == *[[六甲比命神社]] *[[法道仙人]] *[[四鬼氏]] *[[六甲修験]][http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm 六甲修験道の概要ルート] == 出典・注釈 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{デフォルトソート:たもんし}} [[Category:真言系仏教]] [[Category:役小角]] [[Category:別当寺]] [[Category:神戸市の寺]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] [[Category:神戸市北区の建築物]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 吉祥院多聞寺 |画像 = [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |所在地 = 兵庫県神戸市北区有野町唐櫃 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |山号 = 六甲山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]]{{Cite book|和書|author = 有馬郡|authorlink = |title = 有馬郡誌|url = {{NDLDC|1174068}}|format = |accessdate = 2014-01-22|date = 1929-09-10|publisher = |location =|volume = 上|page = 445|chapter = 第三章 寺院|ref = harv}} |寺格 = 高野山[[谷上]]西禅寺末 |本尊 = 毘沙門天

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 吉祥院多聞寺 |画像 = [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |所在地 = 兵庫県神戸市北区有野町唐櫃 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |山号 = 六甲山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]]{{Cite book|和書|author = 有馬郡|authorlink = |title = 有馬郡誌|url = {{NDLDC|1174068}}|format = |accessdate = 2014-01-22|date = 1929-09-10|publisher = |location =|volume = 上|page = 445|chapter = 第三章 寺院|ref = harv}} |寺格 = 高野山[[谷上]]西禅寺末 |本尊 = 毘沙門天
吉祥天
[[善弐師童子]]
|創建年 = [[孝徳天皇]]の頃（645〜654年） |開山 = 伝 [[法道仙人]] |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 六甲山吉祥院多聞寺 |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} '''吉祥院多聞寺'''（たもんじ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[北区 (神戸市)|北区]][[有野町]][[有野町唐櫃|唐櫃]][[有馬郡#近世以降の沿革|有馬郡]][[唐櫃村]]、[[有野村]]{{small|[[大字]]唐櫃}}にある[[真言宗]]の寺院。山号は[[六甲山]]。本尊は秘仏[[毘沙門天]]・[[吉祥天]]・善弐師童子。前立ち本尊は毘沙門天である。 == 歴史 == この寺はインドの渡来僧、[[法道仙人]]によって[[孝徳天皇]]の頃に開かれたと伝えられる。その後、荒廃していた寺院を858年[[高野山]]の僧、長善[[阿闍梨|阿舎利]]が復興した。伽藍は元は現在地の東南にある[[古寺山]]にあった。 1180年に[[平清盛]]が神戸の[[福原京|福原]]に遷都した時、京都の[[鞍馬山]]に擬して都の鬼門（北東）方向を守る寺院とした。北神戸歴史の道　[[野村貴郎]]著　神戸新聞総合出版センター刊[[寺領]]千町歩を与え、鞍馬・[[八瀬 (京都市)|八瀬]]・[[大原 (京都市)|大原]]から人々を移住させたために、この唐櫃地区には近代まで他とは異なる独特の風習が伝わっていた。　 [[治承・寿永の乱]]（源平合戦）の際、[[源義経]]が須磨の一の谷を攻める時、多聞寺がその道案内を断ったことを理由に焼き払われた。その後、廃虚になっていたのを1462年古寺山から現在地に移転・再建された。現在の本堂は1690年に了円が建て直したものである。 かつては毎年2月11日に催される「お塔祭」は盛大に行われていた。このお塔祭をはじめ、[[唐櫃村|唐櫃]]の村を取り仕切るのは[[四鬼家]]であった。四鬼家ははるか昔、[[役行者]]の命によって[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[天川村]][[洞川 (吉野郡天川村)|洞川]]から、この唐櫃に移り住んだといわれ、唐櫃村と西六甲の山を管理し、同時に六甲修験の山伏達を管理していた。 == 多聞寺奥の院 == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|六甲比命神社]] [[六甲山]]山頂尾根付近の仰臥岩・雲が岩（紫雲賀岩）・[[六甲比命神社]]（祭神[[弁財天]]）・心経岩を奥の院とする。雲が岩で法道仙人が修行中、紫色の雲に乗った[[毘沙門天]]と出会った、と伝わる。 == 関連項目 == * [[六甲比命神社]] * [[法道仙人]] * [[四鬼氏]] * [[六甲修験]][http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm 六甲修験道の概要ルート] == 出典・注釈 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{デフォルトソート:たもんし}} [[Category:真言系仏教]] [[Category:役小角]] [[Category:別当寺]] [[Category:神戸市の寺]] [[Category:神戸市北区の建築物]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}}

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 吉祥院多聞寺 |画像 = [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |所在地 = 兵庫県神戸市北区有野町唐櫃 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |山号 = 六甲山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]]{{Cite book|和書|author = 有馬郡|authorlink = |title = 有馬郡誌|url = {{NDLDC|1174068}}|format = |accessdate = 2014-01-22|date = 1929-09-10|publisher = |location =|volume = 上|page = 445|chapter = 第三章 寺院|ref = harv}} |寺格 = 高野山[[谷上]]西禅寺末 |本尊 = 毘沙門天
吉祥天
[[善弐師童子]] |創建年 = [[孝徳天皇]]の頃（645〜654年） |開山 = 伝 [[法道仙人]] |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 六甲山吉祥院多聞寺 |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} '''吉祥院多聞寺'''（たもんじ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[北区 (神戸市)|北区]][[有野町]][[有野町唐櫃|唐櫃]][[有馬郡#近世以降の沿革|有馬郡]][[唐櫃村]]、[[有野村]]{{small|[[大字]]唐櫃}}にある[[真言宗]]の寺院。山号は[[六甲山]]。本尊は秘仏[[毘沙門天]]・[[吉祥天]]・善弐師童子。前立ち本尊は毘沙門天である。 == 歴史 == この寺はインドの渡来僧、[[法道仙人]]によって[[孝徳天皇]]の頃に開かれたと伝えられる。その後、荒廃していた寺院を858年[[高野山]]の僧、長善[[阿闍梨|阿舎利]]が復興した。伽藍は元は現在地の東南にある[[古寺山]]にあった。 1180年に[[平清盛]]が神戸の[[福原京|福原]]に遷都した時、京都の[[鞍馬山]]に擬して都の鬼門（北東）方向を守る寺院とした。北神戸歴史の道　[[野村貴郎]]著　神戸新聞総合出版センター刊[[寺領]]千町歩を与え、鞍馬・[[八瀬 (京都市)|八瀬]]・[[大原 (京都市)|大原]]から人々を移住させたために、この唐櫃地区には近代まで他とは異なる独特の風習が伝わっていた。　 [[治承・寿永の乱]]（源平合戦）の際、[[源義経]]が須磨の一の谷を攻める時、多聞寺がその道案内を断ったことを理由に焼き払われた。その後、廃虚になっていたのを1462年古寺山から現在地に移転・再建された。現在の本堂は1690年に了円が建て直したものである。 かつては毎年2月11日に催される「お塔祭」は盛大に行われていた。このお塔祭をはじめ、[[唐櫃村|唐櫃]]の村を取り仕切るのは[[四鬼家]]であった。四鬼家ははるか昔、[[役行者]]の命によって[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[天川村]][[洞川 (吉野郡天川村)|洞川]]から、この唐櫃に移り住んだといわれ、唐櫃村と西六甲の山を管理し、同時に六甲修験の山伏達を管理していた。 == 多聞寺奥の院 == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|六甲比命神社]] [[六甲山]]山頂尾根付近の仰臥岩・雲が岩（紫雲賀岩）・[[六甲比命神社]]（祭神[[弁財天]]）・心経岩を奥の院とする。雲が岩で法道仙人が修行中、紫色の雲に乗った[[毘沙門天]]と出会った、と伝わる。 == 関連項目 == * [[六甲比命神社]] * [[法道仙人]] * [[四鬼氏]] * [[六甲修験]][http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm 六甲修験道の概要ルート] == 出典・注釈 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{デフォルトソート:たもんし}} [[Category:真言系仏教]] [[Category:役小角]] [[Category:別当寺]] [[Category:神戸市の寺]] [[Category:神戸市北区の建築物]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 吉祥院多聞寺 |画像 = [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |所在地 = 兵庫県神戸市北区有野町唐櫃 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |山号 = 六甲山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]]{{Cite book|和書|author = 有馬郡|authorlink = |title = 有馬郡誌|url = {{NDLDC|1174068}}|format = |accessdate = 2014-01-22|date = 1929-09-10|publisher = |location =|volume = 上|page = 445|chapter = 第三章 寺院|ref = harv}} |寺格 = 高野山[[谷上]]西禅寺末 |本尊 = 毘沙門天

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 吉祥院多聞寺 |画像 = [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |所在地 = 兵庫県神戸市北区有野町唐櫃 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |山号 = 六甲山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]]{{Cite book|和書|author = 有馬郡|authorlink = |title = 有馬郡誌|url = {{NDLDC|1174068}}|format = |accessdate = 2014-01-22|date = 1929-09-10|publisher = |location =|volume = 上|page = 445|chapter = 第三章 寺院|ref = harv}} |寺格 = 高野山[[谷上]]西禅寺末 |本尊 = 毘沙門天
吉祥天
[[善弐師童子]] |創建年 = [[孝徳天皇]]の頃（645〜654年） |開山 = 伝 [[法道仙人]] |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 六甲山吉祥院多聞寺 |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} '''吉祥院多聞寺'''（たもんじ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[北区 (神戸市)|北区]][[有野町]][[有野町唐櫃|唐櫃]][[有馬郡#近世以降の沿革|有馬郡]][[唐櫃村]]、[[有野村]]{{small|[[大字]]唐櫃}}にある[[真言宗]]の寺院。山号は[[六甲山]]。本尊は秘仏[[毘沙門天]]・[[吉祥天]]・善弐師童子。前立ち本尊は毘沙門天である。 == 歴史 == この寺はインドの渡来僧、[[法道仙人]]によって[[孝徳天皇]]の頃に開かれたと伝えられる。その後、荒廃していた寺院を858年[[高野山]]の僧、長善[[阿闍梨|阿舎利]]が復興した。伽藍は元は現在地の東南にある[[古寺山]]にあった。 1180年に[[平清盛]]が神戸の[[福原京|福原]]に遷都した時、京都の[[鞍馬山]]に擬して都の鬼門（北東）方向を守る寺院とした。北神戸歴史の道　[[野村貴郎]]著　神戸新聞総合出版センター刊[[寺領]]千町歩を与え、鞍馬・[[八瀬 (京都市)|八瀬]]・[[大原 (京都市)|大原]]から人々を移住させたために、この唐櫃地区には近代まで他とは異なる独特の風習が伝わっていた。　 [[治承・寿永の乱]]（源平合戦）の際、[[源義経]]が須磨の一の谷を攻める時、多聞寺がその道案内を断ったことを理由に焼き払われた。その後、廃墟になっていたのを1462年古寺山から現在地に移転・再建された。現在の本堂は1690年に了円が建て直したものである。 かつては毎年2月11日に催される「お塔祭」は盛大に行われていた。このお塔祭をはじめ、[[唐櫃村|唐櫃]]の村を取り仕切るのは[[四鬼家]]であった。四鬼家ははるか昔、[[役行者]]の命によって[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[天川村]][[洞川 (吉野郡天川村)|洞川]]から、この唐櫃に移り住んだといわれ、唐櫃村と西六甲の山を管理し、同時に六甲修験の山伏達を管理していた。 == 多聞寺奥の院 == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|六甲比命神社]] [[六甲山]]山頂尾根付近の仰臥岩・雲が岩（紫雲賀岩）・[[六甲比命神社]]（祭神[[弁財天]]）・心経岩を奥の院とする。雲が岩で法道仙人が修行中、紫色の雲に乗った[[毘沙門天]]と出会った、と伝わる。 == 関連項目 == * [[六甲比命神社]] * [[法道仙人]] * [[四鬼氏]] * [[六甲修験]][http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm 六甲修験道の概要ルート] == 出典・注釈 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{デフォルトソート:たもんし}} [[Category:真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:役小角]] [[Category:別当寺]] [[Category:神戸市の寺]] [[Category:神戸市北区の建築物]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Temple in Japan |Name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji |Image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |Location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |Founded= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]]

According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Original pages]][[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Original pages]][[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

revision.comment: fixing the error I made with the apostrophe in the categories. Also fixing ills that were set to |en| instead of |ja| for some reason
revision.text:
{{Temple in Japan |Name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji |Image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |Location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |Founded= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Original pages]][[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

{{infobox religious building |Name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji |Image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |Location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |Founded= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō {{infobox religious building |Name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |Founded= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{infobox religious building |Name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |Founded= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

{{infobox religious building |Name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |Founded= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

{{infobox religious building |Name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |Founded= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

{{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecrated= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

{{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

{{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

{{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

{{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}}{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}
{{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}}
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kobe|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}}, along with {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山}}. In 1180 CE, during {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}}. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]]

Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from {{ill|Kurama|ja|鞍馬}}, {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}}, the temple was burned down by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家}}, who were said to have migrated from {{ill|Tenkawa, Nara|ja|天川村}}, under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|毘沙門天}} here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo}} [[Category:Shingon Buddhism temples]] [[Category:Temples associated with En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:New]]
