Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|qq=}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|qq=}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Betto-ji]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]]

{{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]]{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Zen-ji|qq=}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]]
[[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]]
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō-sennin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]], along with [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|qq=}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|qq=}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]]

According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1078814}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.786833|N|135.205639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17212692]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]]{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Zen-ji|qq=}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]]
[[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]]
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō-sennin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]], along with [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|qq=}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|qq=}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|qq=|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1078814}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.786833|N|135.205639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17212692]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]]{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Zen-ji|qq=|1=Sai Zen-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30946181}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]]
[[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]]
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō-sennin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]], along with [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|qq=|1=Yasse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391538}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|qq=|1=oohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433107}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|qq=|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]]

Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō-sennin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]], along with [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|qq=|1=Yasse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391538}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|qq=|1=oohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433107}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|qq=|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taimon-ji Temple]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1078814}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.786833|N|135.205639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17212692]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]]{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Zen-ji|qq=|1=Sai Zen-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30946181}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]]
[[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]]
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō-sennin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]], along with [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|qq=|1=Yasse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391538}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|qq=|1=oohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433107}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|qq=|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Taimon-ji Temple]] [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q17212692}}

According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q17212692}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1078814}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.786833|N|135.205639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17212692]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]]{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Zen-ji|qq=|1=Sai Zen-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30946181}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]]
[[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]]
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō-sennin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]], along with [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|qq=|1=Yasse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391538}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|qq=|1=oohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433107}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|qq=|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] {{wikidata link|Q17212692}}

It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|qq=|1=Yasse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391538}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|qq=|1=oohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433107}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|qq=|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] {{wikidata link|Q17212692}}

[[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q1078814}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.786833|N|135.205639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17212692]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]]{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Zen-ji|1=Sai Zen-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30946181}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]]
[[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]]
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō-sennin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]], along with [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|1=Yasse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391538}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=oohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433107}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] {{wikidata link|Q17212692}}

The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] {{wikidata link|Q17212692}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q1078814}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.786833|N|135.205639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17212692]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]]{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Zen-ji|1=Sai Zen-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30946181}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]]
[[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]]
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō-sennin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]], along with [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|1=Yasse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391538}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=oohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433107}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] {{wikidata link|Q17212692}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] {{wikidata link|Q17212692}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q1078814}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.786833|N|135.205639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17212692]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name= Kisshō-in Tamon-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tamonji (Temple in Kita-ku, Kobe).jpg|250px|]] |location= {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 47 | Latitude_sec = 12.6 |Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 12 | Longitude_sec = 20.3 |Mountain= Mount Rokkō (六甲山) |Sect= [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]]{{Cite book|和書|author=有馬郡|title=有馬郡誌|url={{NDLDC|1174068}}|date=1929-09-10|location=|volume=上|page=445|chapter=第三章 寺院|ref=harv}} |Subordinate_temple= Sub-temple of {{ill|Sai Zen-ji|ja|西禅寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Zen-ji|1=Sai Zen-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30946181}}, Tani-gami |Main_image= [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]]
[[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]]
Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子) |consecration_year= During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (645–654) |Founder= Tradition attributes to {{ill|Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō-sennin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} |Other_names= }} '''Kisshō-in Tamon-ji''' (吉祥院多聞寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple located in {{ill|Arino-chō, Karato|ja|有野町唐櫃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arino-chō, Karato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:北區|Kita-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The mountain name is Mount Rokkō (六甲山). The principal image of worship is the secret [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]], along with [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and Zen-ni-shidōji (善弐師童子). == History == According to tradition, the temple was founded by the Indian monk {{ill|Hōdō|lt=Hōdō-sennin|ja|法道仙人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]. It was later restored in 858 CE by the Kōyasan monk Chōzen Ajari. Originally, the temple structures were located southeast of the current site on {{ill|Kotera-yama|ja|古寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotera-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1180 CE, during [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}’s relocation of the capital to Fukuhara (modern-day Kobe), the temple was designated to guard the northeastern direction of the city, akin to Kyoto’s [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]]. Taira no Kiyomori endowed the temple with vast landholdings and relocated people from [[:en:Kurama|Kurama]], {{ill|Yase|ja|八瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yase|1=Yasse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391538}}, and {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=oohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433107}}, leaving the Karato region with unique customs that persisted into modern times. During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]], the temple was burned down by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] for refusing to assist in his attack on Ichi-no-Tani. The temple was later rebuilt in 1462 at its present location. The current main hall was reconstructed in 1690 by the monk Ryōen. The temple once hosted a grand annual “Otō Festival” on February 11. The {{ill|Karato Village|ja|唐櫃村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karato Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was historically managed by the {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who were said to have migrated from [[:en:Tenkawa, Nara|Tenkawa, Nara]], under the command of {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役小角|1=En no Gyōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q198946}}. They managed the village and the western Rokkō mountains, overseeing the activities of Rokkō Shugendō practitioners. == Oku-no-in of Tamon-ji == [[File:Rokkohime shrine, Kobe 003.jpg|right|thumb|150px|Rokkō Hime Shrine]] The Oku-no-in (inner sanctuary) is located along Mount Rokkō’s ridgeline near {{ill|Gyōgai-iwa|ja|仰臥岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōgai-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Un-ga-iwa|ja|雲ヶ岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un-ga-iwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Rokkō Hime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Shrine|1=Rokkōhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215573}} (dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}). According to legend, Hōdō-sennin encountered [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Vaiśravaṇa]] here, descending on purple clouds during his ascetic training. == See Also == * {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōdō|ja|法道仙人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法道|1=Hōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555373}} * {{ill|Shiki family|ja|四鬼氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Rokkō Shugendō|ja|六甲修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkō Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20131219044244/http://www.geocities.jp/jyunkubo/kobe/hike_sub/roko_shugen/roko_shugenroute.htm Overview of Rokkō Shugendō] == References == {{Reflist}} {{coordinates |34|47|12.6|N|135|12|20.3|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo}} {{translated page|ja|多聞寺 (神戸市北区)|version=100295545|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:多聞寺 (神戸市北区)]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shingon temples]] [[Category:Temples in Kobe]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kobe]] {{wikidata link|Q17212692}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
